If no Majority is established and a leader is not announced by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from late April, including a nine-point Democratic lead for Roy Cooper over Mike Whatley in North Carolina and Rep. Mary Peltola's edge in Alaska, alongside fundraising advantages in key battlegrounds, have lifted trader consensus on a Democratic Senate flip to favor Chuck Schumer at 35.5% as the presumptive leader. Incumbent Majority Leader John Thune trails at 26%, buoyed by Republican positioning to hold Ohio and flip the open Michigan seat where Mike Rogers leads. Brian Schatz's 15.5% odds capture uncertainty in potential Democratic caucus leadership contests post-flip. The tight race underscores Democrats' need for four net pickups without losses on a tough map; Michigan and Georgia primaries, national approval trends, and further polling could create separation before November 2026.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Patrick Mahomes remains the trader consensus favorite at 61% implied probability to start as Chiefs QB1 in Week 1 of the 2026 NFL season, buoyed by steady rehab progress from his torn ACL suffered in Week 15 last year and public vows to target opening day, echoed by Adam Schefter's expectation of his readiness. Uncertainty persists post-surgery, elevating Justin Fields to 25.5% after Kansas City's March trade acquisition positioned him as depth chart QB2, with Andy Reid praising him as a "legitimate starting NFL quarterback" ready for early-season duty if needed. Practice squad veteran Chris Oladokun garners 25% on system familiarity, while free-agent vets Joe Flacco (22.1%) and Gardner Minshew (15.2%) reflect contingency options amid cap restructuring and post-draft stability including late-round pick Garrett Nussmeier.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US crude oil inventories plunged 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24—the largest draw in months and fifth consecutive weekly decline—fueled by record exports near 5 million barrels per day and refinery inputs averaging 16.1 million barrels per day. This reflects surging US production, strong global demand, and supply tightness from Strait of Hormuz disruptions, with Brent crude prices peaking toward $115 per barrel in Q2 2026 per EIA forecasts. Traders eye weekly EIA Petroleum Status Reports, next on May 6 for data through May 1, culminating in the early June release by June 5 that sets market resolution based on commercial stocks excluding Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Continued large draws hinge on export momentum, refinery utilization, and geopolitical developments.

markets.question: Will AS Monaco FC win on 2026-05-10?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026
If AS Monaco FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AS Monaco FC vs. Lille OSC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between AS Monaco FC and Lille OSC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lille's fourth-place standing in Ligue 1 with 58 points from 32 matches edges out Monaco's sixth-place 54 points, fueling trader consensus for a razor-thin contest despite Monaco's home advantage at Stade Louis II. Lille's recent head-to-head dominance—1-0 win in August 2025 and 2-1 in February—bolsters their implied probability, while Monaco's defensive injuries persist, with fullbacks Vanderson and Caio Henrique sidelined by hamstring issues, alongside absences of Salisu, Ouattara, and Minamino from late April reports. Both sides show mixed recent form, Monaco drawing 2-2 at Toulouse before a 2-1 win at Metz, keeping the market tightly bunched around 35-40% for victory outcomes amid even historical matchups and shared European ambitions.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

This positioning reflects trader consensus on robust Q1 earnings growth—projected at 12% for 2026—bolstered by AI sector strength, against the Federal Reserve's unchanged 3.5%-3.75% fed funds target after the April 28-29 FOMC meeting amid March unemployment holding at 4.3% and moderating inflation. Wall Street year-end targets average around 7,600 (JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs), implying modest 5-6% upside from current levels near all-time highs. Traders eye April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC for signals on potential rate cuts.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' single-lap dominance, securing all poles through Japan with Kimi Antonelli and George Russell, has propelled them to the constructors' lead at 135 points after Miami, yet trader consensus prices every team near even at 49.5% for Canadian Grand Prix constructor pole position amid the 2026 regs' parity push. Circuit Gilles Villeneuve's razor-thin margins, punishing kerbs, and Wall of Champions chicane amplify unpredictability, favoring clean laps over raw pace as track evolution and clean air prove decisive. Ferrari trails closely at 90 points with strong Q3 threats from Leclerc and Hamilton, while midfield risers like Haas and Alpine eye upgrades; newcomers Audi Revolut and Cadillac lag but could surprise in low-downforce setup.

events.title: Mirassol FC vs. Associação Chapecoense de Futebol
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Mirassol FC and Associação Chapecoense de Futebol.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mirassol's unblemished head-to-head record—four straight 1-0 wins over Chapecoense, including both Serie B clashes last year—combined with home advantage at Estádio José Maria de Campos Maia, anchors trader consensus at 65% implied probability for a home win amid both teams' struggles in the early Serie A relegation scrap. Mirassol sit 18th with a 1-3-6 record, hampered by recent losses like 0-1 to Coritiba and 3-2 at Botafogo, while bottom-placed Chapecoense (20th, 1-5-6 form) have drawn heavily but leaked goals in defeats such as 1-4 to Botafogo. Lingering injuries, including Mirassol's Igor Carius (thigh, early May question mark) and Chapecoense's cruciate ligament absences (Mancha, Bruno Matias), temper expectations, yet historical low-scoring dominance favors Mirassol's defensive edge in this table clash.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

events.title: F1 Drivers' Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship with 100 points after three consecutive Grand Prix victories in China, Japan, and most recently Miami on May 3, where he claimed pole and dominated ahead of McLaren's Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri. This rookie sensation's raw pace and consistency have outshone Mercedes teammate George Russell, who sits second on 80 points after his Australia opener but trails in qualifying head-to-heads, driving trader consensus to price Antonelli at 40.9% implied probability versus Russell's 27.5%. Norris at 11.2% reflects McLaren's podium resurgence, while Verstappen (5.3%) and Leclerc (5.1%) face steeper climbs amid Red Bull and Ferrari's early-season deficits in the new regulations era.

markets.question: Will Firecracker die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
markets.description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Boys' fifth and final season, streaming weekly on Prime Video since its April 8, 2026 premiere, has confirmed multiple character deaths through five episodes, anchoring trader consensus amid showrunner Eric Kripke's promise of Survivor-like eliminations. A-Train's early demise in Episode 1 set a brutal tone, Cindy fell in Episode 2, and Firecracker's impalement by Homelander capped the April 29 Episode 5 alongside celebrity supe cameos. Karl Urban teased massive casualties before Episode 7's end credits, fueling speculation on Billy Butcher, Frenchie, Homelander, and The Deep per comic precedents and interviews. Episode 6 drops May 6, with the finale poised to resolve high-stakes fates in Homelander's escalating tyranny.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, a solidly Democratic North Shore Chicago suburb encompassing Evanston and Skokie, favors trader consensus on Democratic nominee Daniel Biss at 92.5% implied probability following his March 17 primary victory over 15 rivals in the open seat race triggered by Rep. Jan Schakowsky's 2025 retirement announcement. Biss, a former state senator and Evanston mayor with strong local fundraising exceeding $250,000 post-primary, faces low-profile Republican John Elleson in the November 3 general election. Historical margins exceeding 30 points for Democrats, absent competitive GOP spending or national midterm wave dynamics, underpin the pricing; upheaval would require a Biss scandal, surged Republican turnout, or unforeseen external shocks like endorsement shifts.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 75.5% implied probability against a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting the April 29 FOMC decision to hold the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%–3.75% amid balanced risks to its dual mandate. March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—up from 2.4%—driven by energy prices, yet unemployment held steady at 4.3% with modest 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains, signaling no overheating. The March dot plot median projects 3.4% by year-end 2026, aligning with futures-implied steady path and dampening hike expectations despite FOMC dissent. Key catalysts include April CPI release on May 12 and the June FOMC meeting.

markets.question: Will Juan Branco win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Jordan Bardella of Rassemblement National leads trader consensus at 23.5% implied probability for the 2027 French presidential election, reflecting recent Harris Interactive polls showing him at 34-35% first-round support amid persistent RN momentum following March municipal elections, where the party was widely perceived as strengthened despite left-wing holds in major cities. Édouard Philippe of Horizons trails closely at 19.5%, bolstered by March polls indicating he narrowly defeats Bardella in runoff scenarios (51-52% to 48%) and rising approval as a centrist alternative. The race remains tight due to a fragmented field—over 30 candidates—with left-wing divisions exacerbated by Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 3 candidacy announcement yielding only 12-13% polling, and no major party primaries yet consolidating support. Separation could arise from Les Républicains' Retailleau endorsement, economic shifts, scandals, or left coalitions ahead of the April 2027 first round.

markets.question: Spread: Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 10:15 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa" if Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Albacete Balompié".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Albacete Balompié vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Albacete Balompié hosts relegation-battling Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa in LaLiga 2 on May 9 at Estadio Carlos Belmonte, with trader consensus likely favoring the mid-table hosts due to home advantage and Leonesa's dismal form. Leonesa sit 22nd after recent defeats, including 2-1 at Mirandés on April 26, 1-2 home loss to Córdoba on April 18, and 1-0 at Granada on April 12, leaving them desperate for points in a must-win survival push amid their 8-8-20 record. Albacete, 13th in the table, lost 0-3 at Eibar on April 24 but won 4-1 versus Granada prior, showing attacking threat at home. Head-to-head history tilts toward Leonesa with two prior wins and three draws, though low-scoring (0.8 goals average); no major injuries reported, but long-term absences like Albacete's Antonio Puertas persist. Late-season stakes heighten upset potential for the visitors.

Independiente Petrolero, mid-table in Bolivia's Primera División with mixed results such as a recent 0-0 stalemate against Guabirá, brings solid away form but faces travel and adaptation challenges. No major injuries or suspensions reported for either side, and limited head-to-head history leaves the matchup closely contested based on trader consensus, with group positioning implications heightening stakes.

markets.question: Will Dylan Beavers win the 2026 AL Rookie of the Year award?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 American League Rookie of the Year award for the 2026 MLB Season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Tigers shortstop Kevin McGonigle holds a slim edge in the AL Rookie of the Year race at 38.5% implied probability, fueled by his AL-leading 1.5 fWAR, .315 batting average, and a 12-game hitting streak through roughly 30 contests, earning him AL Rookie of the Month honors for March/April and top billing in an executive poll just days ago. Chicago White Sox first baseman Munetaka Murakami trails closely at 35.5%, powered by an MLB-leading 13 home runs—including streaks of four and five straight games—despite a .239 average, highlighting his elite raw power from NPB. This tight trader consensus reflects McGonigle's well-rounded contributions versus Murakami's unsustainable homer pace, with Guardians outfielder Chase DeLauter third at 8.5 fWAR behind both, as the crowded field awaits sustained production amid a long season.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 22, 2026, parliamentary election under proportional representation, Robert Golob's Freedom Movement secured a narrow plurality but failed to form a coalition government by April 20, returning the mandate. President Nataša Pirc Musar urged parliament on April 25 to nominate a candidate able to build a National Assembly majority of 46 seats. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), election runner-up, now leads coalition negotiations, bolstered by recent statements insisting on stable governance and reported progress like ministry cuts. Trader consensus prices Janša's success at 89%, reflecting his stronger ties with center-right parties like NSi (led by Jernej Vrtovec at 4%), amid stalled talks and no snap election imminent.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid's league-leading 138 points and sixth Art Ross Trophy have driven trader consensus to price him at 53.3% implied probability for the Hart Memorial Trophy, underscoring his outsized impact carrying the Edmonton Oilers through a competitive regular season. Nikita Kucherov sits at 31.5% after a prolific 130 points in 76 games for the Tampa Bay Lightning, bolstered by voter trackers where he led some mid-season polls with 10 of 16 first-place votes. Nathan MacKinnon's 17.5% reflects his Maurice "Rocket" Richard Trophy with 53 goals and 127 points powering the Colorado Avalanche, though trailing in overall scoring. Macklin Celebrini's 4.2% stems from his standout rookie performance elevating the San Jose Sharks. With Hart finalists due May 8, no major injuries or shifts have altered the post-season stat-driven narrative in the past week.

markets.question: Palantir total customers above 1040 in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Palantir’s total customer count (typically labeled "customer count") for the first fiscal quarter of 2026, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Palantir’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Palantir Technologies reported 1,007 total customers in Q1 2026, up sequentially from 954 in Q4 2025, validating trader consensus extrapolated from prior 34% year-over-year growth and 5% quarter-over-quarter expansion. U.S. commercial customers accelerated to 615, reflecting 42% year-over-year and 8% sequential growth driven by Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) adoption, with 206 deals exceeding $1 million and U.S. commercial remaining deal value surging 112% year-over-year to $4.92 billion. Revenue beat estimates at $1.633 billion, up 85% year-over-year, alongside raised FY2026 guidance to $7.65–$7.66 billion (71% growth) and a Rule of 40 score of 145%, underscoring expanding enterprise monetization ahead of Q2 results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham holds a commanding 83% implied probability as trader consensus for the South Carolina Republican Senate primary on June 9, 2026, bolstered by his Trump endorsement, overwhelming fundraising advantage exceeding $20 million, and strong incumbency in the GOP stronghold. markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Manila be 29°C or below on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ph/manila/RPLL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: PAGASA's latest 5-day forecast projects a 35°C high temperature for Port Area in Metro Manila on May 5, driving trader consensus with 41.5% implied probability for that outcome at the resolving Ninoy Aquino International Airport station, where 35.7°C was observed yesterday amid danger-level heat indices across 13 areas. Prevailing easterlies and partly cloudy skies with a 40% chance of isolated rainshowers position nearby odds—36°C at 30.5% reflecting persistence of recent heat, and 34°C at 29.5% accounting for potential cooling from thunderstorms—while May's climatological norms of 33–36°C highs and urban heat island effects in Manila amplify baseline warmth. Updated PAGASA advisories expected later today could refine model consensus amid inherent short-term forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Exact Score: Everton FC 2 - 2 Manchester City FC?
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Everton FC and Manchester City FC, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Everton FC vs. Manchester City FC match originally scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's defensive injury crisis—Rúben Dias sidelined with a hamstring issue, John Stones nursing a calf problem potentially returning late April, and Joško Gvardiol out long-term—has traders pricing exact score outcomes evenly at 49.5%, underscoring uncertainty for the May 4 Premier League matchweek 35 clash at Everton's Hill Dickinson Stadium. Second-placed City chase Arsenal atop the table with strong recent form including wins over Chelsea and Arsenal, but vulnerabilities expose them to Everton's mid-table resilience, highlighted by a gritty 2-2 draw at Brentford and competitive Merseyside derby loss to Liverpool on April 19. Everton's home advantage and counter-threat balance City's attacking prowess in head-to-head dominance, keeping scoreline probabilities tightly contested amid high-stakes title implications.

events.title: CA Platense vs. CA Peñarol
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for Thursday, May 7, 2026 between CA Platense and CA Peñarol.

eventMetadata.context_description: CA Platense enters as trader consensus slight favorite at 46% implied probability for their Copa Libertadores Group E home clash against Peñarol, buoyed by a 2-1 upset victory in the first leg at Montevideo that vaulted them to second in the standings behind Corinthians. Peñarol's recent domestic losses and mounting injury crisis—key doubts over Ferreira, Darias, and Ángulo, plus long-term absences like Leonardo Fernández (knee) and Nahuel Herrera (shoulder)—have eroded their away threat to 24%, while conserving players for this pivotal fixture underscores its stakes for top-two qualification. Platense plans a near-identical lineup to their winning XI, with only minor tweaks like Gómez for Amarfil, amplifying home form in a closely contested matchup where draw pricing at 30.5% reflects defensive resilience on both sides.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the open Democratic primary for New York's 12th Congressional District—spanning Manhattan's Upper East and West Sides, Midtown—traders view state Assemblymembers Alex Bores and Micah Lasher as co-favorites at equal implied probabilities, reflecting their legislative experience, strong local fundraising, and district ties amid Rep. Jerry Nadler's retirement. Jack Schlossberg's third-place standing stems from his Kennedy family name recognition and viral social media presence, bolstered by a recent first campaign ad, though polls show fluctuating support in a crowded 18-candidate field. Gov. Kathy Hochul's April endorsement of Lasher and April candidate forums at 92nd Street Y have kept the race tight, with no independent polls yet clarifying a frontrunner ahead of the June 23 primary; major union endorsements or ad blitzes could tip the balance in this low-turnout contest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in National Weather Service guidance and ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF, which project Miami International Airport's lowest temperature on May 5 around 73-76°F, with 2-3°F spreads explaining the near-tie among 72-77°F bins at 21-21.5% implied probabilities. Southerly winds of 5-10 mph and partly cloudy skies curb radiative cooling, while urban heat island effects at the airport add 1-2°F warmth above early-May climatological norms of 72°F, under a stable high-pressure ridge. Differentiation hinges on overnight cloud variability—thicker cover trapping heat for 76°F+ versus clearer intervals dipping to 72°F—amid minimal changes in the past 24 hours. Watch 12z model runs and afternoon NWS revisions for potential shifts before resolution.

markets.question: LoL: Disguised vs Dignitas (BO3) - LCS Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Disguised and Dignitas in the LCS Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 9 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Disguised" if Disguised win the match against Dignitas.

This market will resolve to "Dignitas" if Dignitas win the match against Disguised.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Disguised enters this LCS regular season BO3 against Dignitas as the slight favorite among traders, reflecting their recent 2-0 upset win over DIG in Esports World Cup 2026 NA qualifiers that eliminated the opponent. Both squads languish at the bottom of Spring Split standings—Disguised with a 1-4 series record after a 0-3 loss to Team Liquid on May 2, and winless Dignitas reeling from sweeps by Shopify Rebellion and Cloud9 last week. Key factors include stagnant rosters, adaptation to the latest patch meta favoring aggressive early game, and desperation for series points to climb toward playoffs, with head-to-head history split but DSG's qualifier momentum tilting sentiment.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue leads trader consensus at 80.5% implied probability for the Massachusetts Republican gubernatorial primary following his landslide victory at the April 26 MassGOP state convention, where he secured over 70% of delegate votes for the party endorsement and automatic ballot qualification. Former biotech CEO Minogue's outsider appeal as a combat veteran and political newcomer propelled his dominance, while Mike Kennealy suspended his campaign after failing the 15% delegate threshold, dropping his odds to 0.9%. Brian Shortsleeve qualified for the September ballot at 13.5% but faces party pressure to exit, potentially clearing the path for Minogue amid a narrowed field and no recent polls. Upcoming events include candidate filings and early voting trends that could influence the closely watched primary.

markets.question: Gasanova vs. Guo: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Anastasia Gasanova and Hanyu Guo in the Istanbul, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA Challenger statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Istanbul: Anastasia Gasanova vs Hanyu Guo Match O/U 21.5

events.title: Istanbul: Anastasia Gasanova vs Hanyu Guo
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Anastasia Gasanova and Hanyu Guo in the Istanbul, originally scheduled for May 5, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Anastasia Gasanova' if Anastasia Gasanova advances against Hanyu Guo.

This market will resolve to 'Hanyu Guo' if Hanyu Guo advances against Anastasia Gasanova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anastasia Gasanova and Hanyu Guo meet in the WTA 125 Istanbul Open R32 on outdoor red clay, marking their head-to-head debut between mid-ranking players (Gasanova No. 200, 12-9 YTD; Guo No. 167, 12-6 YTD). Gasanova's recent clay form includes a straight-sets semifinal run in an ITF event before a Wiesbaden quarterfinal loss to Julia Grabher five days ago, while Guo advanced to a WTA 125 Huzhou final last week but took an arm medical timeout after slipping versus Diletta Cherubini. No confirmed injuries or withdrawals shape trader consensus, with Guo's higher Elo (1613 vs. 1509) and clay career edge (56% win rate) offsetting Gasanova's fresher surface reps ahead of the May 5 start.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin edge for Elena Pridankina at 50% implied probability in her WTA Istanbul Open round-of-32 clay-court clash with fifth seed Sofia Costoulas, balancing Pridankina's dominant 6-2, 6-3 head-to-head win over Costoulas just two weeks ago at the Oeiras Open CETO against Costoulas's superior No.151 ranking to Pridankina's No.229. Pridankina arrives with momentum from recent clay victories, including straight-sets triumphs over Kamilla Rakhimova and Rebeka Masarova, while Costoulas seeks redemption after a quarterfinal exit to Moyuka Uchijima in Saint-Malo. No injury concerns reported; late surface adaptation or fatigue from qualifiers could tip the scales on outdoor clay.

The competitive balance stems from Dutra da Silva's convincing straight-sets win over Mena in the Campinas Challenger last April on similar clay, bolstering his surface history, plus home-crowd support amid ten Brazilians in the main draw announced yesterday. Recent form shows both with mixed 2026 results—Dutra da Silva's Sarasota upsets offset by a Savannah qualifying loss, Mena's San Luis Potosi win preceding a Santa Cruz exit—while late withdrawals or weather could sway odds pre-match.

Sinner's red-hot clay form—capped by his Madrid Masters 1000 title over Alexander Zverev last week, extending a 28-match win streak at the level—bolsters trader consensus, despite pressure for a career Grand Slam. Zverev (6.2%) and Djokovic (6.6%) trail as primary challengers, leveraging strong historical clay results and experience, while French hopeful Arthur Fils (3.9%) and rising Spaniard Rafael Jodar (3.3%) draw niche support amid a thin field. Draw details emerge soon ahead of the May 24 start.

West Ham, battling mid-table security with a gritty 2-1 home win over Everton on April 25 and a resilient 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace days earlier, leverage London Stadium's fortress-like atmosphere and solid home form against goal-shy opening periods. Title frontrunners Arsenal, who reclaimed Premier League summit via a narrow 1-0 victory versus Newcastle on the same weekend, face injury headwinds including Bukayo Saka's ankle issue, Martin Ødegaard's knee problem, and doubts over Declan Rice and Jurrien Timber, tempering their attacking fluency early. Head-to-head history shows tight first halves, amplifying the even-market dynamics.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Eastern Conference semifinals, the No. 7 seed Philadelphia 76ers carry momentum from a dramatic 3-1 comeback over the top-seeded Celtics into their series against the No. 3 seed New York Knicks, who hold home-court advantage with a dominant 30-10 record at Madison Square Garden. Joel Embiid's probable listing for right hip contusion—stemming from a physical Game 7 win—remains the pivotal injury report factor, compounded by his recent appendectomy recovery, while Tyrese Maxey is available despite a finger strain; Knicks forward Jeremy Sochan is questionable with a hamstring issue. Regular-season head-to-head split 2-2 underscores a tight matchup, with Knicks' guard play and frontcourt depth posing key challenges for Philly's stars amid rest advantages and playoff intensity.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16, 2026, Democrat Analilia Mejia defeated Republican Joe Hathaway by approximately 20 points in initial results, securing Mikie Sherrill's former House seat for Democrats in this suburban battleground. Pre-election polling and the district's Democratic lean, bolstered by Mejia's progressive endorsements from Bernie Sanders and AOC, fueled expectations of a comfortable win, reflected in trader consensus pricing a 20-25% margin at 96.7%. Low special election turnout favored the Democratic base, narrowing the GOP House majority. Final certification awaits provisional ballots and absentees, with slim odds of recount-driven shifts below 20% absent major discrepancies.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This tempers earlier secondary market pricing at $1.48 trillion in April amid xAI merger synergies boosting prior tenders from $800 billion in December 2025. Surging Starlink revenue and Starship milestones drive optimism, but execution risks, regulatory scrutiny, and equity market volatility cap higher brackets. Key catalysts include SEC feedback and potential mid-June listing, with resolution hinging on final prospectus terms.

markets.question: Will South Carolina use a new congressional map for the 2026 United States midterm elections?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed US state uses a new congressional district map for the 2026 United States midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

To qualify, after the 2024 United States congressional elections, the listed state must have adopted a new congressional district map that is:
- Formally adopted and enacted into law by the appropriate legislative or redistricting authority;
- Not enjoined, vacated or otherwise fully struck down prior to the 2026 United States Midterm elections; and
- In effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are temporarily stayed pending appeal but later upheld will qualify if they are in effect for use in the 2026 United States midterm elections.

Maps that are completely redrawn by a court, special master, or legislature before implementation will not qualify, unless the redrawn map is itself a new map used in the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If the 2026 United States midterm elections occur and no new qualifying map is in use, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the listed state and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: An unprecedented wave of mid-decade congressional redistricting has seen seven states—California, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Texas, Utah, and Virginia—enact new maps since late 2025, driven by partisan legislatures and commissions seeking House advantages for the November 2026 midterms. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis signed a legislature-dominant map on May 4, 2026, shifting districts toward Republicans, while a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on April 29 struck Louisiana's map as a racial gerrymander, prompting primary delays and redraw plans. Ongoing Virginia Supreme Court hearings over referendum legality and special sessions called in Tennessee and Alabama heighten uncertainty, as trader consensus reflects enacted changes versus lingering litigation risks before candidate filing deadlines.

markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and May 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

eventMetadata.context_description: U.S. retail gasoline prices have surged to a national average of approximately $4.46 per gallon as of May 4, per AAA data, driven primarily by a sharp rally in crude oil benchmarks—WTI at $105 per barrel and Brent above $113—amid escalating Middle East tensions disrupting supply routes like the Strait of Hormuz. Recent EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Reports show declining gasoline production and tightening inventories, exacerbating upward pressure as summer driving season approaches with Memorial Day travel boosting demand. Trader consensus on prediction markets reflects this volatility, with key swing factors including weekly EIA inventory releases every Wednesday and potential OPEC+ output decisions; prices could ease if tensions de-escalate or inventories rebuild, but sustained geopolitical risks favor further gains before May 31 resolution.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 73% implied probability for U.S. enactment of an AI safety bill before 2027, driven by a divided 119th Congress showing limited progress on comprehensive federal AI regulation amid partisan gridlock and a Trump administration emphasis on innovation over stringent safety measures. The White House's March 2026 National AI Legislative Framework urged light-touch rules focused on child protections, infrastructure permitting, and preemption of state laws, but no major safety bill has advanced beyond committee introductions like the bipartisan GUARD Act on child chatbot harms, which cleared Senate Judiciary unanimously last week. Recent announcements, such as Sanders and Ocasio-Cortez's AI Data Center Moratorium Act, face steep hurdles with midterms looming and historical low passage rates for AI bills, reinforcing skepticism on timely enactment.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

This surge from December 2025's $800 billion tender offer reflects Starlink's explosive subscriber growth, Starship reusability milestones enabling higher launch cadence, and global direct-to-cell spectrum deals expanding addressable markets. Upcoming S-1 prospectus by mid-May and roadshow could catalyze further shifts, though execution risks like regulatory hurdles persist amid high hype.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro at 61.7% as Venezuela's head of state by end-2026, reflecting the Supreme Court's January ruling classifying his U.S. capture as a "forced absence" that preserves his official title despite custody in New York, where he pleads not guilty. Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in as interim president with military backing, holds 23.5% amid recent purges of Maduro allies—like the Central Bank chief replacement—and cabinet reshuffles to consolidate chavismo control under U.S. oversight, though no elections have materialized despite opposition demands. María Corina Machado's 8.0% stems from her planned return and calls for polls, but institutional barriers limit momentum in the transitional limbo. Upcoming U.S.-Venezuela talks on elections could shift probabilities.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Guangzhou be 29°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/guangzhou/ZGGG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest numerical weather prediction models, including those from the China Meteorological Administration and global ensembles like ECMWF and GFS, show a tight consensus for Guangzhou's highest temperature on May 6 clustering around 28-30°C, driving trader sentiment toward these outcomes at 70% combined implied probability. This reflects current subtropical high-pressure ridging over southern China, with warm air advection from the north tempered by sea breezes from the South China Sea and elevated humidity near 80%, limiting peaks above 31°C. Differentiating factors include model spread on afternoon cloud cover and isolated convective showers—more sunshine favors 30°C, while increased precipitation supports 28°C—against May climatological averages of 29°C highs. New 12-hourly model updates expected by May 5 could refine this uncertainty ahead of resolution based on official observations.

markets.question: Will Portugal win on 2026-06-17?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 17, 2026
If Portugal wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Portugal vs. DR Congo
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Wednesday, June 17, 2026 between Portugal and DR Congo.

eventMetadata.context_description: Portugal's overwhelming squad depth and elite talent, including a recovered Cristiano Ronaldo alongside Bruno Fernandes and Rafael Leão, drive the 75.5% trader consensus for victory in this FIFA World Cup Group K opener against DR Congo on June 17 at NRG Stadium. The Seleção's recent Nations League success and March friendlies wins over USA (2-0) and a draw with Mexico underscore their form, even without Ronaldo initially sidelined by hamstring strain. DR Congo's historic qualification via Axel Tuanzebe's late playoff header against Jamaica (1-0 on March 31)—their first since 1974—fuels 8.5% upset hopes with compact defending led by Chancel Mbemba and Yoane Wissa's pace, but talent gap sustains the 15.5% draw pricing amid potential counter threats in Houston's June heat.

markets.question: Will Khamenei post 40-44 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Earlier April markets resolved around 10-14 posts, but three weeks without updates signal trader caution on resumption.

markets.question: Will Airbnb (ABNB) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Airbnb is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Airbnb's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.30 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Airbnb reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.30 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Airbnb releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 64% implied probability that Airbnb (ABNB) will not beat Q1 2026 consensus estimates of $0.30 EPS and $2.62 billion in revenue, due primarily to Zacks' negative Earnings Surprise Prediction (ESP) score signaling limited upside potential. This reflects softening occupancy trends reported by host analytics firm AirROI amid moderating travel demand, despite average daily rate (ADR) gains of 11-37%, which align with Airbnb's tight $2.59-2.63 billion revenue guidance issued in February. Q4 2025 results showed revenue growth of 12% but an EPS miss from tax charges, tempering optimism. Key catalyst: earnings release on May 7, with focus on nights booked and gross booking value growth.

markets.question: Will JD Gaming qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).
